reviews.review_msg: Exams were lengthy!!!

Great Prof!! Nice & Understanding!! But the course has way too many things to be memorized. When there are too many concepts, you start to get confused. Therefore, do practice on the study guide!! That's pretty helpful!

His lectures are ok, nothing spectacular but does have a few somewhat funny stories now and then. He wrote the text, so his lectures give the same info as the text, so just read the book. Exams are quite tricky, and the class is not curved.

His class was just OK.Actually he taught a lot of interesting stories, but the voice was not so clear, therefore made me want to sleep.But the text is really clear,and helpful.Do all the fact questions in the workbook,it's really helpful for the exam.
Waste of money and time, i dunno how people can actually reading the text it's so painful

The ratings don't do him justice. He's to the point, very fair and amusing. A lot of ppl don't appreciate his humor though. Lectures are NOT boring as he fills them with relevant stories. The only thing is the fact pattern question marking scheme. If you know how to approach it, its cake. If not, you're screwed on a huge part of the exams

Hard class, but the textbook which he wrote is clear and if you don't read it you won't do well!!  He's got some good stories that help explain a lot of difficult laws, but sometimes the lecture can get a bit boring near the end.

Really interesting class. Lectures are boring as hell (unless you like sleeping). Read the textbook from cover to cover in order to do well. His marking is somewhat arbitrary, especially with the presentations. Law is awesome.
This class is tough. Professor Yates wrote the book so everything in it is testable. Lectures are definitely not mandatory but he makes them interesting so it's not too bad if you go to them.
ok

Lectures are very boring.  Slept through EVERY one, no lie.  But since he wrote the text, it is very relevant.  The notes are great as well.  My friend never opened his text and still came out with a b+.

Jordan is great at explaining concepts with clear examples. His concise slides make the material easy to follow. The course had fair exams (70%), a straightforward group presentation (20%), and participation (10%). He was super accessible and kind - even responding to emails all-day before exams. Highly recommend!

One of my favourite instructors I've had so far, James makes sure to keep the class as engaged as possible by providing examples from his own life as a lawyer and even provide hints on how to do well in his exams. To succeed in his class, get ready to do lots of practice case questions.

So James lectures are based out of the modules he presents, you will have to read through text material if you are looking for good grades. He's definitely able to create a real connection of the law with examples from corporate business structures. There's a lot to memorize, think like a corporate lawyer.

Easily my favourite professor throughout 4 years of Beedie. Hilarious, like laughing out loud in class hilarious. 

There is a lot to memorize, you need to read the textbook every week. But James' lectures & anecdotes will clear any confusion.

For the students who say he is sometimes rude - it's not malicious - keep in mind that Pflanz is first and foremost a real practicing lawyer, and second, a lecturer at SFU. He deals mostly with real-world legal issues and actual clients, whereas SFU keeps their students in a bubble. It comes from a place of wisdom, experience, and some humor :)
good prof. lectures enjoyable but disorganized. no point really taking notes just sit back and try to absorb the material. midterm/final were long but marked fairly.

Funny and relatable. However, he does not follow his powerpoints or textbook at all. It can be very disorganized and confusing. He did give us the option of an take home exam or in class, and we chose take home. The grading criteria is very vague and ambiguous. Tough to actually satisfy.

Robin gives interesting anecdotal lectures, but does not go over important key terms. He also changes his test criteria based on student feedback. Constantly adjusting our expectations (open book? closed book?) was very stressful. I'd urge him to just decide one thing and stick to it.

Awesome professor. I would love to take a class with him again. Explains the concepts very properly. The examples he uses in the class are great. He is very clear about what he expects in the midterm and final exams.

Very interesting prof.He is a real world attorney which makes the cases he discusses insightful.Approachable and draws on the book heavily. Overall, it's great to have a prof who can relate the theoretical concepts to real world applications.

Has no intention of helping students.  I went to his office hours 2 days before the midterm and he was not there, so I emailed him and asked if he would be in his office the next day.  He replied to me 20 minutes before the midterm saying he's in his office.  He also rarely responds to emails.

Good prof, explains things clearly. Lectures are recorded and notes are online and are the ones provided by the publisher of the text book. Midterm was easy and final was fine. Don't have to attend lecture if you don't need to but the class is interesting so it's worth while.

The course is crammed with tons of content and sometimes gets off schedule, but Bateman clearly explains the concepts and gives an interesting lecture. He tells lots of humorous stories and presents many cases that help relate the concepts to real-life situations. The workload is fairly light as well. One of my favorite classes in university.

The class is really interesting and I thought coming in that Law would be all about memorization, it's not. Bateman's a good lecturer and the exams are fair and I would say crammable in a day or two if you're really behind.
He is an excellent professor and very nice. He inspired me more to become a lawyer. In 4 years I will be a lawyer because of this course!

boring lectures especially nearing the end of the semester. at first it was pretty interesting with all the stories he tells, but then the stories took too long and we were falling behind so he rushes things. we were expected to know the stuff he didnt cover in class because there wasnt enough time. the exams were hard, have to get every detail

Excellent prof, I attended all his classes and he was very clear on all the materials. The course had no memorization, just need to understand the material and you will be fine. This course made me want to go to Law school, A++.

Excellent prof, I attended all his classes and he was very clear on all the materials. The course had no memorization, just need to understand the material and you will be fine. This course made me want to go to Law school, A++.
friendly, extremely intelligent and helpful - he's the best prof. fantastic course - made me want to be a lawyer :) use the study guide!
ok
well our midterm is two weeks from now and so far i love his lecture, very much!!
had him as a TA...was ok. marks hard compared to yates. sort of unclear when it comes to the presentation, but explains law stuff pretty well.
very nice prof, clear about the topic, always share useful personal experience. Midterm and Final are hard. a lot of stuff to study.
Great instructor. Knows lots about Business Law. The course itself isn't easy... lots of memorizing. I don't know how Yates is like but I recommend taking BUS 393 with Bateman.
good prof, hard material. lots of memorizing. very responsive to help. i don't know what yates is like, but bateman is highly recommended for this course
No Comments

He likes to brag about being a practicing lawyer. The exams are ridiculously difficult. avg was 62% in my class and these are business students I'm talking about. His lectures are very boring and he has trouble e-mailing the lecture slides.

He's a good prof that brings his experience in to the classroom. He uses examples through his experiences, which enhances learning. He also has a pretty good attitude towards students and seems willing to help. I would definitely recommend 393 with him.
Bus 393 was a special topics class that Colin made fun and interesting.

Amazing professor. Good sense of humor. He genuinely cares about his students. Tests are tricky but as long as you study you'll be fine. One of my favorite classes taken. Definitely recommend you to take this course with him!
Colin is a great prof! He is extremely nice and helpful, and his lectures were always interesting. Awesome guy and awesome prof, I highly recommend taking any class with him!
Nice guy, course was very interesting. Always provided good examples. Would take this course again with new cases. Open book exams are tough as its all based on your judgements. Great class.

Colin is extremely nice and always available for help. Exams were open book. But, they weren't more difficult and the questions were straight-forward, no trick questions. Lectures weren't really useful, but the material made the course that way. Also, it is VERY IMPORTANT to read the textbook. This class is not as boring or hard as it seems.

He is not meant to be a teacher. 2 days before the midterm I went to his office hours and he wasnt there, so I emailed him, and he replied to me 20 mins before the start of the midterm saying he's in his office now (literally). Although he does explain concepts clearly, the exams are hard.

John is a very engaging prof. He brings in real world examples to demonstrate examples. Very approachable, and his tests are fair provided you read the text and attend lectures. Lots of vital material presented that is relevant to post university endeavors.

He taught law in an interesting way, and exams (while all written Qs) clearly tested what he talked about in lecture. Only assignment is a presentation-analysis on a topic of your choice. Do well by taking notes on the cases he talks about, and by reviewing the slides for anything else he skips. Use Casebook only for cases he mentions in class.
The exams were all short-answer, in a short time period. Be ready to write fast and hope your hand doesn't cramp. The tutorials made learning the content fun and pretty easy.
Exams are very fair, and the annotated lecture slides and casebook are all you need to succeed in the course. Both of the TAs for this course were great as well.

Absolutely loved him. Most useful class beedie has to offer. After the first lecture, I was genuinely interested in the content and he relates it to real life examples. 100 min 100 question midterm and final which was straight forward if you go through his voice recording slides. 20 min tut presentation was not bad at all. 10/10 prof

Robert was a great prof. He had slides with audio on each one to explain further. Exams were 100 marks and 100 minutes, so around 80 questions, and one group presentation around 20 minutes, which can be a bit crazy, but they were fair and no surprises and clear rubric. No textbook, all you need to succeed are the slides. Best option to take.
Pretty good prof. However, he talks too slow. Please talk faster! Record the whole lecture as a video rather than voice messages on powerpoint.

Robert is such a fun guy and just an overall fantastic prof. He really cares about his students. There was 1 midterm, and in-class final, and tutorial presentation. The exams were pretty fair and if you read  and listen to his annotated slides, you should do well in the course
Really nice guy and took a lot of his time to help out students

course was fun because Robert tries to keep it light and interactive. Crazy exams though. 100 marks 100 mins but you also need to read cases to questions. Robert makes some amazing slides. Has his voice recording explaining what's happening in each slide, thats helpful. Tutorial presentations were chill

You don't need the textbook or to read the slides, just use the tutorial slides and the practice midterm given by the TA's to study. Both TAs are fantastic. His exams were so easy. Beware of high curve, almost everyone i know got curved down. Shafik as a prof is good but overhyped, his lectures are not that useful, tends to cover 4 slides a lect.
Shafik is a gem. Best professor I've ever had. Exams are a cakewalk. Don't really need the textbook. TAs provide plenty of review material so as long as you drill that there will be 0 issues.

HE IS THE BEST PROF IN BEEDIE NGL
he will make everything easy, and he will give you many examples to make the concepts not that tough. 
Personally think he is the only prof worth me to pay such international tuition
Shafik is great! Pretty easy class, just study lecture/tutorial slides, do any practice exams provided and you'll do fine.

Shafik's lectures are super interesting and he gives lots of good examples that stick with you for the exams (so you should attend lecture). The class is fairly easy with a light workload for a 300-level class but the tradeoff is high a average so you have to score high to get above the curve. Shafik was awesome and hope I can have him again.

Taken 2 classes with him 393 & 303. Hands down, great prof. Super consistent, always prepared, & makes the material make sense. He doesn't just read off slides. Used tons of real-life examples. Also really funny, keeps the class from ever getting boring. But heads up — don't use your phone in his class.

Shafik made lectures enjoyable. His exams are also very fair. The group project could use more structure/instructions. Will make plenty of jokes which I loved (not for the easily offended). Teaches using examples, which is how you will be evaluated. Will take more courses with him even though I don't need to.
Shafik is a great prof! He is a funny guy and teaches well. Exams are fair as well, just wish you're not on his bad books and don't get bullied every lecture.
